posts.caption:
Anthropic, Microsoft in talks for AI chip deal after $5 billion investment - Part 1

Microsoft has not made the Maia 200 chips available to customers, but they are used in the company's data centers, offering better efficiency than other silicon.

Key takeaways:
- Anthropic, Microsoft in talks for AI chip deal after $5 billion investment Anthropic is in talks with Microsoft to adopt its Maia artificial...
- Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei recently said the company has had “difficulties with compute.” Microsoft is in talks to supply its custom artificial...
- The company did say the Maia 200 processor would run OpenAI’s GPT-5.2 model.
- Microsoft announced its second-generation Maia AI chip in January, but has yet to make it available through its Azure cloud.

Source: cnbc.com | Curated by Graitech AI.

posts.caption:
Anthropic, Microsoft in talks for AI chip deal after $5 billion investment - Part 2

Learn how agent harnesses transform AI models into autonomous work engines. Explore core components: filesystems, sandboxes, and memory.

Key takeaways:
- The model contains the intelligence and the harness makes that intelligence useful.
- From a Model's Perspective There are things we want an agent to do that a model cannot do out of the box.
- The Anatomy of an Agent Harness Break down complex objectives: Planning tools let agents decompose tasks, track progress, and adapt as they learn...
- Orchestration Logic (subagent spawning, handoffs, model routing) Hooks/Middleware for deterministic execution (compaction, continuation, lint...
